reason: Browser-facing QA usually creates validation and browser evidence artifacts.
record_when: The workflow opens, inspects, captures, validates, or writes durable QA evidence.

reason: Specialist review workflows create retained review findings for broad or risky diffs.
record_when: The workflow runs specialist review passes, writes a report, or gates a PR/release decision.

reason: Quick review can create retained findings or escalation evidence for a diff.
record_when: The workflow records findings, residual risk, or an escalation decision.

reason: Focused lens review can create specialist findings for a selected risk area.
record_when: The workflow runs a forced specialist lens, writes findings, or gates a decision.

reason: Pre-merge review gates affect delivery readiness and should retain the review outcome.
record_when: The workflow runs a pre-merge gate, writes a gate result, or blocks/allows delivery.

reason: Review follow-up records whether previous findings were resolved or accepted.
record_when: The workflow classifies previous findings after repairs or records disposition evidence.

reason: GitHub PR creation is provider-side delivery state.
record_when: The workflow creates or materially prepares a GitHub pull request.

reason: GitHub PR delivery spans validation, checks, provider state, and merge handling.
record_when: The workflow creates, updates, watches, readies, merges, or closes a GitHub pull request.

reason: GitHub PR close/merge changes provider-side delivery state.
record_when: The workflow marks ready, merges, closes, or cleans up a GitHub pull request.

reason: GitLab MR creation is provider-side delivery state.
record_when: The workflow creates or materially prepares a GitLab merge request.

reason: GitLab MR delivery spans validation, pipelines, provider state, and merge handling.
record_when: The workflow creates, updates, watches, readies, merges, or closes a GitLab merge request.

reason: GitLab MR close/merge changes provider-side delivery state.
record_when: The workflow marks ready, merges, closes, or cleans up a GitLab merge request.

reason: Dispatch lane PR creation links plan execution state to provider delivery state.
record_when: The workflow creates or materially prepares an assigned dispatch lane pull request.

reason: Automated CI repair can modify code, push commits, and iterate on remote checks.
record_when: The workflow inspects or fixes failing GitHub Actions checks.

reason: Release workflows change public version, tag, changelog, and distribution state.
record_when: The workflow prepares, validates, publishes, or repairs a release.

reason: Autonomous bug-fixing can scan, edit, validate, commit, and open PRs.
record_when: The workflow scans for bugs, applies fixes, validates, or opens a bug PR.

reason: Bug PR repair changes existing provider-visible remediation state.
record_when: The workflow fixes, updates, comments on, or resolves an existing bug PR.

reason: Security automation can scan, triage, edit, and open report or fix PRs.
record_when: The workflow runs Semgrep triage, fixes findings, or opens a security/report PR.

reason: Issue follow-up turns discovered work or blockers into durable provider state.
record_when: The workflow opens, updates, or continues a GitHub issue for durable follow-up.

reason: Dispatch PR review ties provider comments, findings, issue state, and merge decisions together.
record_when: The workflow reviews a PR through issue-linked evidence, comments, or merge decisions.

reason: Dispatch issue closeout reviews subagent lanes, merges PRs, and closes plan-issue runtime work.
record_when: The workflow reviews, merges, or closes existing issue-backed dispatch work.

reason: Dispatch subagent PR work owns an implementation lane and provider PR handoff.
record_when: The workflow implements an assigned lane, creates a draft PR, or responds to review.

reason: Dispatch plan delivery coordinates sprint execution, subagent PRs, and acceptance gates.
record_when: The workflow dispatches, reviews, merges, or advances plan-driven issue sprints.

reason: Plan creation writes durable implementation artifacts that guide later execution.
record_when: The workflow creates or updates a durable docs/plans implementation bundle.

reason: Dispatch plans add sprint, PR lane, and subagent execution metadata.
record_when: The workflow creates or updates a dispatch-ready execution-modeling plan.

reason: Plan execution performs implementation work against a durable execution-state ledger.
record_when: The workflow resumes, edits, validates, or updates execution state from a plan.

reason: Parallel plan execution dispatches subagents, integrates changes, and validates results.
record_when: The workflow dispatches, integrates, validates, or updates state for plan tasks.

reason: Plan cleanup removes or reconciles durable coordination artifacts.
record_when: The workflow audits, edits, removes, or reconciles docs/plans artifacts.

reason: Durable artifact cleanup removes obsolete implementation artifacts after execution.
record_when: The workflow audits, removes, rehomes, or reconciles durable implementation artifacts.

reason: Runtime skill refreshes can mutate live Codex or Claude homes and should keep validation evidence visible.
record_when: The workflow runs or materially prepares scripts/sync-runtime-skills.sh with --apply.

reason: Repo-owned skill lifecycle changes affect future agent behavior and should be traceable when invoked.
record_when: The workflow scaffolds, edits, validates, or commits a new runtime-kit skill.

reason: Project-local skill lifecycle changes affect future agent behavior in a consuming repo.
record_when: The workflow scaffolds, edits, validates, or commits a new project-local skill.

reason: Skill removal is a breaking lifecycle change and should have retained context.
record_when: The workflow removes a skill directory or purges repo references.

reason: Project-local skill removal changes future agent behavior in a consuming repo.
record_when: The workflow removes a project-local skill directory or purges project-local references.
